content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 24,
1992
Richard L. Mansfield
City Clerk
4455 West 126th Street
Hawthorne,
CA
90250
RE:
Combined Campaign Report
Our File No.
A—92-34l
Dear Mr. Mansfield:
This is to confirm advice provided to your office on June 15,
1992,
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).l/
This letter constitutes
written advice under Section 84205.
Section 84205 states:
The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required
to be filed by this title.
A ballot measure qualification committee will be submitting
petitions to your office on June 26,
1992, to place a measure on
the November 3,
1992, ballot.
The campaign statement due in
connection with the qualification effort (Section 84200.5)
would
normally cover July 1,
1992,
through July 10,
1992, with a filing
deadline of July 17,
1992.
However,
we believe it is appropriate
for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the
qualification of the ballot measure to combine that report with
the semi-annual statement for the first six months of 1992
(Section 84200).
Therefore,
such committees may file a single
1/
Government Code Section 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916)322-5660


Richard L. Mansfield
Our File No.
1—92—341
Page
2
campaign statement disclosing contributions received and
expenditures made during the period January
1 through July 10,
1992.
The deadline for this combined report is July 17,
1992.
If you have any questions regarding the above,
please do not
hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Ma Ann Kvasager
Li
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: May ballot measure qualification committees combine their campaign statement for the qualification effort with the semi-annual statement for the first six months of 1992?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, committees primarily formed to support or oppose ballot measure qualification may file a single combined campaign statement covering January 1 through July 10, 1992, with a deadline of July 17, 1992, pursuant to Section 84205 authority.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; question and conclusion reconstructed from letter body. Letter confirms prior oral advice given June 15, 1992. Written advice under Section 84205 authority.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May ballot measure qualification committees combine their campaign statement for the qualification effort with the semi-annual statement for the first six months of 1992?

CONCLUSION: Yes, committees primarily formed to support or oppose ballot measure qualification may file a single combined campaign statement covering January 1 through July 10, 1992, with a deadline of July 17, 1992, pursuant to Section 84205 authority.

Mansfield City Clerk 4455 West 126th Street Hawthorne, CA 90250 RE: Combined Campaign Report Our File No. A—92-34l Dear Mr. Mansfield: This is to confirm advice provided to your office on June 15, 1992, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).l/ This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205. Section 84205 states: The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title. A ballot measure qualification committee will be submitting petitions to your office on June 26, 1992, to place a measure on the November 3, 1992, ballot. The campaign statement due in connection with the qualification effort (Section 84200.5) would normally cover July 1, 1992, through July 10, 1992, with a filing deadline of July 17, 1992. However, we believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of the ballot measure to combine that report with the semi-annual statement for the first six months of 1992 (Section 84200). The deadline for this combined report is July 17, 1992. If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Ma Ann Kvasager Li Political Reform Consultant
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that ballot measure qualification committees may combine their required qualification campaign statement with the semi-annual statement into a single filing covering January 1 through July 10, 1992.
